AC1.evidence: Snapshot flow: app.js:578-589 computes hasErrors from rsync_errors/scan_ok, toggles progress-fill--warn on #ns-progress-fill and callout--warn. Workspace flow: app.js:888-898 does the same for copy_errors and shows the count. Re-inspected after the AC3 fix; untouched.

AC2.evidence: rerunSearch closure (app.js:1185-1188) runs performSearch only when query is non-empty; wired to mode (1189-1191), scope (1192-1194), kind (1195-1198), space (1199-1201). Re-inspected; untouched.

AC3.evidence: escapeHtml (app.js:65-69) now appends .replace(/"/g, '"').replace(/'/g, ''') to the div.innerHTML serialization, exactly the fix prescribed in the prior problems.md. Attribute sites verified: #f-title/#f-cid/#f-jobid value="..." (app.js:762-764), #cd-prid (1431), #cd-jira (1460), #cd-hours (1464), #cd-live-date (1469), #cd-qa-job (1475), plus title= (655), data-path (954, 1243-1244), href (1742) all interpolate inside double-quoted attributes; " decodes back to a literal quote in element.value, so values containing quotes survive re-render and the attribute-injection vector is closed. Roundtrip is stable: input handlers mirror the decoded raw value back to state (bundleForm 776-780, changeDocs 1528-1540, promptInput 1354-1356), and escapeHtml is always applied to the raw state value at render time, so no double-escaping accumulates. Textareas #prompt-input (1321), #cd-qa-items (1479), #cd-extra (1484): "/' in element content render as literal characters; correct.

AC4.evidence: _validatePrid (app.js:1654-1667) tests /^\d{14,}$/ with distinct inline messages in #cd-prid-error; changeDocsPreview (1671) and changeDocsGenerate (1685) return before any API call when invalid; error clears on PRID input (1535-1538). Re-inspected; untouched.

AC5.evidence: showModal(title, content, copyText = null) (app.js:89-96) hides #modal-copy when copyText is null and wires copyToClipboard otherwise; previewFile passes raw un-numbered content; showKnowledgeModal calls with 2 args. Re-inspected; untouched.

AC6.evidence: files_copied as Number(finalResult.files_copied) || 0 (app.js:894); copy error count via Number(copyErrors) (895); rsync error count via Number(...) (583). Re-inspected; untouched.

AC7.evidence: 6-column header without Captured; freshness cell carries title="Captured: ..." (app.js:655) — now also quote-safe via the AC3 fix. grid-template-columns with 6 entries (style.css:1171-1174); responsive variant unaffected. Re-inspected; untouched.

AC8.evidence: Use button: disabled + 'Using…' + row .is-busy before the request (app.js:678-681); finally block restores all three on success and failure (697-701). Re-inspected; untouched.

checks.pytest: uv run pytest -q — 200 passed in 0.87s (rerun by verifier, 2026-06-11)

fix_safety_review.all_call_sites: Every escapeHtml call site sits inside template literals assigned via innerHTML (element content or double-quoted attributes). In element content "/' render as literal "/'; in attributes they decode on parse. No textContent = escapeHtml(...) usage and no nested escapeHtml(escapeHtml(...)) anywhere (grep verified), so no double-escaping is introduced.
fix_safety_review.highlightSnippet: app.js:1267-1271 regex-matches the raw query against escapeHtml output; a query containing a literal quote no longer highlights inside snippets, and a query like 'quot' can mark inside the entity. This is the same pre-existing cosmetic edge that already applied to &/</> before the fix; display-only, not a regression of any AC.

scope.notes: Only change since the prior FAIL verdict is the one-line escapeHtml extension at app.js:68; all line anchors cited in the previous verdict still match. Working-tree diffs to server.py/pyproject/uv.lock belong to other tasks (critical-fixes-v1/medium-fixes-v1/changedocs-web), not ux-fixes-v1.

out_of_scope_observations: Pre-existing search Preview index mismatch (app.js:1235 renderHit per-section index vs app.js:1277 lookup against the full state.searchResults array): when both knowledge and file sections are non-empty, Preview opens the wrong hit. Untouched by ux-fixes-v1; worth a follow-up task.

state.changeDocs initial object lacks explicit prid/jira/hours/liveDate/qaJob/qaItems keys; templates compensate with ?? '' so behavior is correct, but declaring the keys would make the state shape explicit.
